descriptions: Jr Earth Angels is a junior version of Earth Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Like the Black Angels, they eventually formed a Jr version, with the Jr Earth Angels' age bracket being in their early 20s.

Organizacion de Narcotraficantes Unidos.description: Puerto Rican criminal organization based in Bayamón, founded in 1995 by José López Rosario. It unified drug dealers under one umbrella, becoming the most powerful drug trafficking organization in Puerto Rico by 2009. Involved in trafficking heroin, cocaine, marijuana, crack, Xanax, and Percocet. Declined after mass arrests in 2009-2010.

descriptions: La ONU is listed as an ally of the Puerto Rican Mob in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Ñetas , American Mafia , Cali Cartel (defunct) , Guadalajara Cartel (defunct) , Latin Kings , Russian mafia , Los Pepes (defunct) , La ONU

descriptions: Bin Laden Records is a local gang that was part of La ONU.
edges.evidence_quote: La ONU was divided into local gangs such as Bin Laden Records, los Extranjeros, los Anormales, among others.

descriptions: La Rompe ONU is a rival organization engaged in a violent war against ONU.
edges.evidence_quote: La Rompe ONU ("ONU Breakers") is a rival organization which is also devoted to the drug trade in Puerto Rico. Rompe ONU was first organized by several former ONU members and has been involved in a violent war against ONU members.

descriptions: los Anormales is a local gang that was part of La ONU.
edges.evidence_quote: La ONU was divided into local gangs such as Bin Laden Records, los Extranjeros, los Anormales, among others.

descriptions: los Extranjeros is a local gang that was part of La ONU.
edges.evidence_quote: La ONU was divided into local gangs such as Bin Laden Records, los Extranjeros, los Anormales, among others.

descriptions: Infobox lists La ONU as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La ONU

descriptions: La ONU is listed as an ally of Ñetas in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Ejército Popular Boricua (sin confirmar), Familia Martinez Sangeros, La ONU

Cali Cartel.description: Colombian drug cartel based in Cali, founded by the Rodríguez Orejuela brothers and José Santacruz Londoño after breaking from Pablo Escobar in 1988. At its peak (1993-1995), it controlled over 80% of the world's cocaine market and was considered the most powerful criminal organization globally. The cartel operated through a decentralized cell structure and was dismantled by the mid-1990s.

descriptions: Big Circle Gang formed an alliance with the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Big Circle Boys in Toronto under the leadership of Tse Chi Lop ... was said to have formed an alliance with ... the Columbian Cali Cartel

descriptions: Cali Cartel allied with Camorra for cocaine distribution in Europe.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cali cartel established a strategic alliance with the powerful Camorra criminal organization. Cali supplied the cocaine and the Camorra handled distribution across Europe.

descriptions: Cartel de Cali supplied cocaine to the Camorra for distribution in Europe.
edges.evidence_quote: Cali suministró la cocaína y la Camorra se encargó de la distribución en Europa.

descriptions: Cartel de la Costa is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cartel de la Costa

descriptions: Cali Cartel split from Medellín Cartel in 1988.
edges.evidence_quote: They broke away from Pablo Escobar and his Medellín associates in 1988

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed Medellín Cartel as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Medellín Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: The cartels became rivals as Medellín encroached on Cali's territory and blamed Cali for Ochoa's arrest.
edges.evidence_quote: By 1987, the cooperation forged by the formation of MAS no longer existed. Contributing to the demise was the Medellín Cartel's Rodríguez Gacha, who attempted to move in on the New York City market, previously ceded to the Cali Cartel, and the 1986 arrest of Jorge Ochoa at a police roadblock, which the Medellín Cartel deemed suspicious and attributed partly to the Cali Cartel.

descriptions: Cali Cartel formed Los Pepes to fight the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: both the Norte del Valle Cartel and Cali Cartel formed and operated a vigilante group called Los Pepes ... to bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar

descriptions: Cali Cartel funded Los Pepes to eliminate their rival Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Calí Cartel provided $50 million to pay for weapons, informants, and assassins, with the hopes that they could wipe out their primary rival in the cocaine business.

descriptions: The Cartel de Cali and Cartel de Medellín were armed rivals starting in the mid-1980s.
edges.evidence_quote: El nombre de « cartel » se le dio principalmente en contraste con el así llamado Cartel de Medellín , quien fue su principal rival, a partir del enfrentamiento armado que tuvieron estas dos organizaciones a mediados de la década de 1980.

descriptions: El Cartel de Cali era competidor del Cartel de Medellín.
edges.evidence_quote: los cabecillas del Cartel de Cali, quienes para la época eran los máximos competidores del Cartel de Medellín en el narcotráfico

descriptions: The cartels cooperated by dividing US distribution territories.
edges.evidence_quote: The two cartels divided up the major United States distribution points: the Cali Cartel took New York City and the Medellín Cartel took South Florida and Miami; Los Angeles was left up for grabs.

descriptions: Cartel de Cali and Cartel de Medellín were allies until 1987.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Medellín (hasta 1987)

descriptions: Cartel de Bogotá is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cartel de Bogotá

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed Norte del Valle Cartel as a rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Norte del Valle Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Cartel del Norte del Valle became an enemy of Cartel de Cali from 1995.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel del Norte del Valle (A partir de 1995).

descriptions: Cali Cartel initially allied with Norte del Valle Cartel, later became rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Norte del Valle Cartel (later became rivals) (defunct)

descriptions: Cartel del Norte del Valle was originally a branch or cell of Cartel de Cali until its separation in 1995.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel del Norte del Valle (brazo o célula del Cártel de Cali que operaba en el norte del Valle del Cauca , hasta su separación en 1995).

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed Guadalajara Cartel as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Guadalajara Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Matta Ballesteros connected Cali and Guadalajara cartels, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue el principal responsable de la conexión colombo-mexicana entre el Cartel de Medellín , Cartel de Cali y Cartel de Guadalajara

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed Gulf Cartel as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Gulf Cartel

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel del Golfo

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed Juarez Cartel as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Juarez Cartel

descriptions: Cártel de Juárez allied with Cartel de Cali to traffic cocaine to the US West Coast.
edges.evidence_quote: El Cártel de Juárez liderado Amado Carrillo Fuentes , se alió con el Cartel de Cali para introducir cocaína a la costa Oeste de los Estados Unidos

descriptions: Cali Cartel helped form Los Pepes to target Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: helped form the vigilante group "Los Pepes"

descriptions: Cali Cartel financed Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was largely financed by the Cali Cartel and led by brothers Carlos and Fidel Castaño whom were right-wing paramilitary leading commanders that were actually once part of the Medellín Cartel.

descriptions: Cali Cartel funded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Calí Cartel provided $50 million to pay for weapons, informants, and assassins, with the hopes that they could wipe out their primary rival in the cocaine business.

descriptions: Cartel de Cali funded Los Pepes to fight Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: el Cartel de Cali abrió su ataque inyectando de efectivo los bolsillos de « Los Pepes », organización criminal conformada por exmiembros del Cartel de Medellín y paramilitares, constituida para acabar con Escobar.

descriptions: Los Pepes were financed by the Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: el grupo de Los Pepes , financiado por el Cartel de Cali y los paramilitares

descriptions: El Cartel de Cali financió a Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes ... fue un grupo narcoparamilitar colombiano conformado por narcotraficantes ex-socios de Pablo Escobar y financiado principalmente por la cúpula del Cartel de Cali

descriptions: Cali Cartel leader Herrera was a founding member of Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Herrera is believed to have been a founding member of Los Pepes

descriptions: Cali Cartel founded Los Pepes to target Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Cali Cartel grew in strength, eventually founding Los Pepes, or Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar ("People Persecuted by Pablo Escobar"). Los Pepes was specifically formed to target the Medellín Cartel and bring about the downfall of Pablo Escobar.

descriptions: Cali Cartel co-founded MAS with Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: the Medellín and Cali cartels, as well as associated traffickers, formed the group Muerte a Secuestradores (MAS; "Death to Kidnappers").

descriptions: Cali Cartel was a founding member of MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: Cártel de Sinaloa is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Sinaloa

descriptions: Cali Cartel listed The Extraditables as an ally.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: The Extraditables (defunct)

descriptions: Cártel de Tijuana is listed as an ally of Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Tijuana

descriptions: Cartel de Buga and Cartel de Cali were armed rivals in the mid-1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: El nombre de " cartel " se le dio, principalmente, en contraste con el así llamado Cartel de Cali , quien fue su principal rival, a partir del enfrentamiento armado que tuvieron estas dos organizaciones a mediados de la década de los 90s.

descriptions: The infobox lists Cartel de Cali as an enemy until its dismantlement.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cartel del Norte del Valle , Cartel de Cali (Hasta su desarticulación)

descriptions: Cartel de Cali assigned Caribbean routes as a territorial franchise to the Costa cartel's leader.
edges.evidence_quote: Al caer el cartel de Medellín, el cartel de Cali asignó las rutas del Caribe como franquicia territorial a Orlández Gamboa.

descriptions: Cali Cartel cooperated with authorities against Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: It also played a role in the manhunt that led to the death of Pablo Escobar and helped form the vigilante group "Los Pepes"

descriptions: Medellín and Cali Cartels became enemies from 1988 until Medellín's demise in 1993.
edges.evidence_quote: by 1988 (supposedly after the Monaco Apartment bombing) the Medellín and Cali organizations became perpetual enemies, a feud which continued until Medellín's eventual demise in 1993.

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel battled rival cartels, including the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: He quickly became one of the richest people in the world, but constantly battled rival cartels domestically and abroad

descriptions: Cali Cartel had recordings of Escobar's wife and demanded reparations for Escobar's war against them.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Cali Cartel even replayed their recordings of her conversations with Pablo for their wives to demonstrate how a woman should behave.

descriptions: Cali Cartel bombed Escobar's apartment building in 1988.
edges.evidence_quote: The building was initially built for Escobar's wife but was gutted by a Cali Cartel car bomb in 1988

descriptions: The Cali Cartel contracted mercenaries to assassinate Pablo Escobar, leader of the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Killing Escobar was a documentary televised in the UK in 2021. It concerned a failed attempt by mercenaries, contracted by the Cali Cartel and led by Peter McAleese, to assassinate Escobar in 1989.

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel fought a fierce war against the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Se enfrentó en una cruenta lucha contra el Estado colombiano y sus instituciones principalmente en centros urbanos, simultáneamente mantuvo feroces guerras, contra el cartel de Cali y otros grupos paramilitares del Magdalena Medio.

descriptions: The Cartel de Medellín waged fierce wars against the Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Se enfrentó en una cruenta lucha contra el Estado colombiano y sus instituciones principalmente en centros urbanos, simultáneamente mantuvo feroces guerras, contra el cartel de Cali y otros grupos paramilitares del Magdalena Medio.

descriptions: The Cartel de Medellín fought fierce wars against the Cartel de Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: mantenía feroces guerras, contra el cartel de Cali

descriptions: Medellín and Cali Cartels had a strategic alliance and smuggling arrangement early on.
edges.evidence_quote: Traditionally, Pablo and the Medellín Cartel originally had a strategic alliance and specific smuggling arrangement with the Cali Cartel 'godfathers' Gilberto Rodríguez Orejuela and Miguel Rodríguez Orejuela early on.

descriptions: Medellín Cartel and Cali Cartel cooperated to form the paramilitary group MAS.
edges.evidence_quote: At the end of 1981 and the beginning of 1982, members of the Medellín Cartel, Cali Cartel, the Colombian military, the U.S.-based corporation Texas Petroleum, the Colombian legislature, small industrialists, and wealthy cattle ranchers came together in a series of meetings in Puerto Boyacá and formed a paramilitary organization known as Muerte a Secuestradores

descriptions: The Cali Cartel was an ally of the Medellín Cartel until 1987.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: ... Cártel de Cali (Hasta 1987)

descriptions: Matta Ballesteros connected Medellín and Cali cartels, indicating cooperation.
edges.evidence_quote: Fue el principal responsable de la conexión colombo-mexicana entre el Cartel de Medellín , Cartel de Cali y Cartel de Guadalajara

descriptions: Cartel del Dorado's leader had drug trafficking relations with Cartel de Cali despite enmity between Medellín and Cali.
edges.evidence_quote: Perafán tuvo relaciones narcos tanto con el cartel de Medellín como el de Cali, a pesar de la enemistad entre ambos.

descriptions: The infobox lists the Cali Cartel as a rival of the Norte del Valle Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Cali Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: The Cartel del Norte del Valle considered the Cali Cartel an enemy.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Cártel de Cali

descriptions: Both cartels cooperated to form Los Pepes against the Medellín Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: both the Norte del Valle Cartel and Cali Cartel formed and operated a vigilante group called Los Pepes

descriptions: The Norte del Valle Cartel emerged after the fragmentation of the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: It rose to prominence during the 1990s, after the Cali and Medellín Cartels fragmented

descriptions: The Cartel del Norte del Valle split from the Cali Cartel in late 1993.
edges.evidence_quote: El Cartel del Norte del Valle se independizó totalmente de su similar de Cali a finales del 1993 luego de la muerte de Pablo Escobar y antes de la captura de los hermanos Miguel y Gilberto Rodríguez Orejuela , jefes del Cartel de Cali .

descriptions: The infobox lists Cali Cartel as allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Cali Cartel

descriptions: The clan acted as intermediary between the Cali Cartel and 'Ndrangheta.
edges.evidence_quote: Caruana brought together the cocaine suppliers of the Cali Cartel with the Italian distributors from the 'Ndrangheta in Calabria.

descriptions: Warren's cartel established direct supply links with the Cali Cartel for cocaine trafficking.
edges.evidence_quote: Forging direct links with the Cali Cartel , Warren and his gang flooded Europe with drugs.

descriptions: Cali Cartel is listed as an ally of Guadalajara Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tijuana Cartel , Medellín Cartel , Cali Cartel , Norte del Valle Cartel , Sinaloa Cartel , Juárez Cartel , Sonora Cartel

descriptions: Cártel de Guadalajara worked with Cártel de Cali to traffic cocaine from Colombia to the US.
edges.evidence_quote: Félix Gallardo comenzó a trabajar con los cárteles colombianos , empezando con el cartel de Cali y el cartel de Medellín liderado por Pablo Escobar .

descriptions: Gulf Cartel negotiated with Cali Cartel for cocaine shipments in the 1980s-1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: By negotiating with the Cali Cartel, García Ábrego was able to secure 50% of the shipment out of Colombia as payment for delivery

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo negotiated with the Cali Cartel for cocaine shipments.
edges.evidence_quote: Al negociar con el Cartel de Cali . Dirigido en esa época por Juan García Abrego , quien asumió el control total de la organización de manos de su tío, Juan Nepomuceno Guerra .

descriptions: Montreal Hells Angels conspired with Cali Cartel and Rizzuto crime family to smuggle cocaine.
edges.evidence_quote: Pierre Rodrigue and David Rouleau, two Canadian Hells Angels from the Sherbrooke chapter, were arrested by British police in London at the request of the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) in February 1995 before being extradited to Canada and sentenced to fourteen years' imprisonment for conspiring to smuggle 558 kilograms of cocaine into the UK in a scheme also involving the Rizzuto crime family and the Cali Cartel.

descriptions: Amado Carrillo Fuentes, head of Juárez Cartel, cooperated with the Cali Cartel in drug smuggling.
edges.evidence_quote: Later, Carrillo worked with Pablo Escobar and the Cali Cartel smuggling drugs from Colombia to Mexico and the United States.

descriptions: Juárez Cartel and Cali Cartel were allies.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: La Línea (lead faction), Beltrán-Leyva Cartel (defunct), Barrio Azteca, Los Zetas, Oaxaca Cartel (defunct), Cali Cartel (defunct)

descriptions: Cártel de Juárez allied with Cartel de Cali after Medellín's decline.
edges.evidence_quote: se asociaron con el Cartel de Cali

descriptions: Los Charlines are allied with the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Medellin Cartel , Cali Cartel , Clan del Golfo , Corleonesi clan , Nuova Camorra Organizzata , Sinaloa Cartel

descriptions: Cali Cartel provided financial support to Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: The group was financed by the Cali Cartel

descriptions: Los Pepes allied with Cartel de Cali against Pablo Escobar.
edges.evidence_quote: en 1992 los Castaño habían formado parte de Los Pepes (Perseguidos por Pablo Escobar), en alianza con el Cartel de Cali , enemigos de Pablo Escobar

descriptions: El Cartel de Cali financió a Los Pepes.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Pepes ... financiado principalmente por la cúpula del Cartel de Cali

descriptions: The Puerto Rican Mob had strong connections with the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: They had strong connections with The Cali Cartel

descriptions: The Estrella Family had strong connections with the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: They had strong connections with The Cali Cartel

descriptions: Sinaloa Cartel obtains cocaine from Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Other shipments of South American cocaine are believed to originate directly from Cali and Medellín drug-trafficking groups in Colombia, from which the Sinaloa Cartel then handles transportation across the U.S. border

descriptions: Sonora Cartel received cocaine shipments from the Cali Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: The Sonora Cartel is believed to be one of the earliest cartels to begin shipping cocaine from Colombia , particularly from the Cali Cartel .

descriptions: Cali Cartel is listed as an ally of the Sonora Cartel in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Medellin Cartel , Cali Cartel , Guadalajara Cartel

descriptions: Tambov gang leader Kumarin was connected to Cali Cartel via money-laundering scheme.
edges.evidence_quote: the German firm St. Petersburg Real Estate Holding Company ... had fallen under suspicion of being involved in a money-laundering scheme through the Cali cocaine cartel with connections to Kumarin.

descriptions: Tijuana Cartel allied with Cali Cartel for cocaine business in the 1990s.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s they formed an alliance with the Cali Cartel and Norte del Valle Cartel for the cocaine business.

descriptions: Listed as ally in infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación , Cartel del Norte del Valle , FARC-EP , Cártel de Cali , Unión Tepito , Mafia China

descriptions: Cártel de Cali provided protection to members of Cártel de Tijuana.
edges.evidence_quote: huyeron a Colombia y recibieron protección por parte de miembros del asociado Cártel de Cali

descriptions: West End Gang partnered with Cali Cartel to transport cocaine.
edges.evidence_quote: Under Ross' leadership, the West End Gang formed a partnership with the Cali Cartel, transporting large quantitites of cocaine into Canada from the United States.

descriptions: The West End Gang partnered with the Cali Cartel to transport cocaine into Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: Under Ross' leadership, the West End Gang formed a partnership with the Cali Cartel, transporting large quantitites of cocaine into Canada from the United States.

descriptions: Bullshit MC was formed by a merger of Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden.
edges.evidence_quote: Bullshit MC formed during the late 1970s in Tårnby, Amager as the result of a merger between the Filthy Few and Nøragersmindebanden

descriptions: Head Hunters are allies with Filthy Few.
edges.evidence_quote: the Filthy Few

descriptions: The Valle clan is allied with the Lampada clan.
edges.evidence_quote: alleati dei Lampada

descriptions: Zavettieri and Pangallo-Maesano-Favasuli-Verno were in conflict in the Roghudi feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Roghudi, negli anni '90 a Roghudi scoppia una faida tra gli Zavettieri e i Pangallo-Maesano-Favasuli-Verno

'Ndrina Franco.description: The 'Ndrina Franco is a 'ndrina of the 'Ndrangheta based in Reggio Calabria, Italy, primarily active in Pellaro and Lazzaro. It is involved in drug and arms trafficking, contract control, and extortion rackets. The group is allied with the Barreca clan. Founded by Giovanni Franco, it has been active since at least the 1990s and remains operational.

descriptions: The Franco clan is an ally of the Barreca.
edges.evidence_quote: Il 7 novembre 2013 viene arrestato per traffico di droga ad Antibes in Costa Azzurra Giovanni Franco esponente di spicco dell'omonima famiglia e alleato al clan Barreca

descriptions: The 'Ndrina Franco-Spanti is allied with the Cosca Barreca.
edges.evidence_quote: alleata con la cosca Barreca , storico clan di Pellaro

descriptions: Varacalli and Franco were allied against Barillaro-Romano-Zucco in the Ciminà feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Faida di Ciminà : a partire dal 4 giugno 1966 ... vengono coinvolte le famiglie Barillaro-Romano-Zucco da una parte, e i Varacalli-Franco-Polifroni-Spagnolo dall'altra.

descriptions: Lopers 13 and Logan Street are rivals.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Walnut street, Logan street

Peckham Boys.description: Multi-generational gang based in Peckham, South London, founded in the 1990s. Known for popularising road rap music and involvement in drug trafficking, arms trafficking, robbery, and murder. The gang operates in Peckham, Walworth, and Camberwell, and is divided into subsets by age group. Active as of 2025.

descriptions: Ghetto Boys and Peckham Boys were in a violent conflict during the 2000s.
edges.evidence_quote: The gang had a notably violent conflict with the Peckham Boys during the 2000s

descriptions: The two gangs agreed to a ceasefire and alliance by 2007/8.
edges.evidence_quote: By 2007/8, after decades of conflict, the Peckham Boys and Ghetto Boys had agreed to a ceasefire and alliance.

descriptions: PDC had a rivalry with Peckham Boys, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The PDC continued Brixtons longtime rivalry with neighbouring Peckham by committing robberies in the area, causing conflict with local gang Peckham Boys.

descriptions: ABM is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Brooklyn is listed as a rival of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Brooklyn, GAS Gang , Ghetto Boys , OC (Organised Crime) , Roadside G's

descriptions: CWS is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Firehouse Crew is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Peckham Boys and Ghetto Boys engaged in a shootout.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2004, the Peckham Boys and Ghetto Boys had an infamous shootout outside the Urban Music Awards

descriptions: Peckham Boys attacked a man mistaken for Ghetto Boys member.
edges.evidence_quote: On 17 September 2006, a man was shot and stabbed to death on the Woodpecker Estate in New Cross by the Peckham Boys, after being mistaken for a member of the Ghetto Boys.

descriptions: Exchange of shots between Ghetto Boys and Peckham Boys members.
edges.evidence_quote: In October 2008, during an exchange of shots between a member of the Ghetto Boys and a Peckham Boys member, Polish care worker Magda Pniewska was hit by a stray bullet and died.

descriptions: PYG and Lettsom Boys engaged in a shootout.
edges.evidence_quote: In April 2014, a man was sentenced to a minimum of 35 years in prison for the shooting of Rio MacFarlane in Peckham. The victim was an innocent bystander in a shootout between the PYG and Lettsom Boys.

descriptions: OC Gang and PYG Gang engaged in a street battle.
edges.evidence_quote: In July 2009, a 13-year-old boy was stabbed five times in Camberwell by the OC (Organised Crime) Gang, in a street battle with the PYG Gang from Peckham.

descriptions: OC gang attempted to murder PYG youths.
edges.evidence_quote: In September 2009, a member of the Brixton-based OC gang was jailed for shooting a bystander dead, when attempting to murder two youths from the PYG gang.

descriptions: OC is listed as a rival of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Brooklyn, GAS Gang , Ghetto Boys , OC (Organised Crime) , Roadside G's

descriptions: OTB is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Roadside G's is listed as a rival of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Brooklyn, GAS Gang , Ghetto Boys , OC (Organised Crime) , Roadside G's

descriptions: PYG and SI merged to form Anti GMG.
edges.evidence_quote: Crane Block (located in Pelican Estate, Peckham) and the Lettsom G'z (located in Lettsom Estate, Peckham) split away from PYG and Shoot Instant (SI). The latter two would end up merging into Anti GMG

descriptions: T-Block is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Wooly Road Youngers is listed as an ally of Peckham Boys in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: CWS, T-Block , ABM (All Bout Money), Firehouse Crew, OTB, Wooly Road Youngers

descriptions: Muslim Dost's group merged to form ISIS–K.
edges.evidence_quote: ISIS–K, when it was officially declared in January 2015, was the product of the merger of Tehrik-e Khilafat Khorasan (TKK), Khilafat Afghan, Muslim Dost's group, Azizullah Haqqani's group, and Tehrik-e Khilafat Pakistan (TKP)

Papalia crime family.description: The Papalia crime family is a 'Ndrangheta organized crime family based in Hamilton, Ontario, Canada, founded by Antonio Papalia in the 1940s. It is one of three centralized Mafia organizations in Hamilton, with strong connections to the Buffalo crime family. The family was decimated after the killings of boss Johnny Papalia and his lieutenant in 1997, ordered by the Musitano family. The last leader, Frank Papalia, died in 2014.

descriptions: The text states the Buffalo family has strong connections with the Papalia family.
edges.evidence_quote: The Buffalo family is purported to hold strong connections with the Hamilton-based Luppino and Papalia families.

descriptions: The Buffalo crime family had influence over the Papalia crime family in Canada.
edges.evidence_quote: The Bonanno crime family and the Buffalo crime family also had influence in several factions in Canada including the Rizzuto crime family and Cotroni crime family, and the Luppino crime family and Papalia crime family, respectively.

descriptions: The Buffalo family influenced the Papalia family in Canada.

edges.evidence_quote: Nel 1997, la famiglia ha ordinato i colpi di mob di Johnny Papalia e Carmen Barillaro.

descriptions: Papalia crime gang is an enemy of Musitano family.
edges.evidence_quote: The enemies included 'criminal groups in Hamilton, Buffalo, Montreal and elsewhere, including the Luppino and Papalia crime gangs,' according to the National Post.

descriptions: The Musitano family is one of three centralized crime families in Hamilton, alongside Luppino and Papalia.

descriptions: After Johnny Papalia's murder, a former Papalia associate switched allegiance to the Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: Reflecting the new power structure, Gerald "Skinny" Ward of Welland, who had long served as the Papalia's family drug distributor and smuggler in the Niagara Peninsula area, went over to the Hells Angels after Johnny's murder.

descriptions: The Musitano family is listed as a rival of the Papalia family in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Musitano crime family and various gangs in Hamilton

descriptions: The Musitano family ordered the murder of Johnny Papalia and his lieutenant, leading to the decline of the Papalia family.
edges.evidence_quote: The killings of Johnny and his lieutenant Carmen Barillaro in 1997, ordered by the Musitanos, had effectively wiped out the family's remaining leaders in Canada.

descriptions: The Papalia family borrowed money from the Rizzuto family and failed to repay, leading to the murder of a Papalia lieutenant.
edges.evidence_quote: In the 1990s, Papalia lieutenant Enio "Pegleg" Mora borrowed $7.2 million from Montreal mob boss Vito Rizzuto, and gave the bulk of the money to Papalia to open an upscale restaurant and nightclub in Toronto. After the Rizzuto crime family were not re-paid, in September 1996, Mora was shot in the head four times at a Vaughan farm; Giacinto Arcuri was arrested and charged with Mora's murder, but was acquitted for lack of evidence.

descriptions: Native Mob is a rival of Native Gangster Disciples.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Native Gangster Disciples , Folk Nation , Gangster Disciples , Native Disciples , Project Boyz , Moe Mob

descriptions: South Side Compton Crips members shot at a vehicle they believed was from Lime Hood Piru Bloods, indicating rivalry.
edges.evidence_quote: The two gang members believed they were protecting a drug house from a rival gang, the Lime Hood Piru Bloods.

edges.evidence_quote: the Jalisco New Generation Cartel under the name Los Matazetas did massacres in the states of Veracruz and Tamaulipas with their intention to remove the rival Los Zetas Cartel.

Los Ciclones.description: Los Ciclones, also known as the Facción Matamoros, is a Mexican criminal organization and one of the four main factions of the Gulf Cartel. Based in Matamoros, Tamaulipas, it was founded between 2010 and 2012 by Rafael Cárdenas Vélez. The group is engaged in drug trafficking, murder, kidnapping, extortion, and money laundering, and operates primarily in northeastern Mexico and the border area with Brownsville, Texas. It is currently active and in conflict with rival cartels such as the Cartel del Noreste and Los Metros.

descriptions: Los Ciclones is listed as an enemy of the Cártel del Noreste in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: ... Los Chapitos , Cártel Jalisco Nueva Generación , Los Metros , Cártel de Caborca , Los Ciclones , Cártel de Juárez , La Familia Michoacana , Zetas Vieja Escuela

descriptions: Los Escorpiones served as the armed wing of the Gulf Cartel.
edges.evidence_quote: Los Escorpiones became the armed wing of the entire Gulf organization.

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Ciclones as a criminal group.

Formby Gang.description: Early Chicago juvenile street gang formed in the late 1890s by teenagers Jimmy Formby, Bill Dulfer, and David Kelly. The gang committed hundreds of burglaries and armed robberies, and later engaged in murder for hire. It dissolved in 1904 after its leaders were imprisoned.

descriptions: The Izmaylovskaya gang has good relations with the Podolskaya gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Izmailovskaya has good relations with the Podolskaya gang.

Lashkar-e-Taiba.description: Pakistani Islamist jihadist militant organization founded in 1985–1986 by Hafiz Saeed and others with funding from Osama bin Laden. Its primary objective is to merge Kashmir with Pakistan and seeks destruction of India, Hinduism, and Judaism through jihad. Designated as a terrorist group by the UN and many countries, it has carried out major attacks in India including the 2008 Mumbai attacks. It is supported by Pakistan's ISI and operates primarily in Kashmir.

descriptions: Lashkar-e-Taiba is allied with al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: The following are presently believed to be allied with al-Qaeda: - Lashkar-e-Taiba

descriptions: Lashkar-e-Taiba is considered a member of al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: Lashkar-e-Taiba is a member of al-Qaeda.

descriptions: D-Company forged relationships with Lashkar-e-Taiba.
edges.evidence_quote: has 'forged relationships with Islamists, including Lashkar-e-Taiba and al-Qaeda.'

descriptions: LeT provides support to al-Qaeda.
edges.evidence_quote: While the primary focus for the Lashkar is the operations in Indian Kashmir, it has frequently provided support to other international terrorist groups. Primary among these is the al-Qaeda Network in Afghanistan.

edges.evidence_quote: The ISYF, along with Babbar Khalsa International (BKI), had reportedly maintained ties with Pakistan’s Inter-Services Intelligence (ISI) and received training and logistical support from Islamist groups like LeT since the early 2000s.

descriptions: Lashkar-e-Taiba allied with IS-KP.
edges.evidence_quote: But Lashkar-e-Taiba , which have allegedly close ties to Pakistan's intelligence agency, have allied with IS-KP and ended their allegiance to Mullah Hibatullah.

descriptions: LeT and JeM jointly carried out the 2001 Indian Parliament attack.
edges.evidence_quote: News reports, citing security forces, said that the latter suspect that on 13 December 2001 attack on India's Parliament in New Delhi, a joint group from the LeT and the Jaish-e-Mohammed (JeM) were involved.

descriptions: LTTE conducted arms transfers and made deals with LeT in the early 1990s.

edges.evidence_quote: The outfit had claimed that it had assisted the Taliban militia and Osama bin Laden's al-Qaeda network in Afghanistan during November and December 2002 in their fight against the US-aided Northern Alliance.

edges.evidence_quote: In response to the UN resolution and the government ban, the JuD reorganized itself under the name of Tehreek-e-Tahafuz Qibla Awal (TTQA).

19th of April Movement.description: Colombian urban guerrilla group active from 1974 to 1990, founded after alleged electoral fraud. It advocated revolutionary nationalism and democratic socialism, and was the second largest guerrilla group in Colombia after FARC. The group demobilized in 1990 and transitioned into a political party, the M-19 Democratic Alliance. Many of its leaders were later assassinated, but former member Gustavo Petro became President of Colombia in 2022.

descriptions: M-19 was allied with Alfaro Vive, Carajo!
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: ¡Alfaro Vive, Carajo!

descriptions: M-19 kidnapped a relative of a Medellín Cartel founder, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: the two organizations had several standoffs and confrontations, like the kidnapping of Nieves Ochoa, the sister of Medellin cartel founder Juan David Ochoa Vásquez, by M-19.

descriptions: M-19's kidnapping led to the creation of MAS by the Medellín Cartel, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: The kidnapping led to the creation of the MAS/ Muerte a Secuestradores ("Death to Kidnappers") paramilitary group by the Medellin cartel.

descriptions: M-19 was allied with FSLN.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Sandinista National Liberation Front

descriptions: M-19 was allied with Tupamaros.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Tupamaros

descriptions: M-19 was allied with MRTA.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Túpac Amaru Revolutionary Movement

descriptions: The text presents conflicting claims: some allege cooperation, others note conflict (kidnappings). The dominant tie is conflict due to standoffs and kidnappings.
edges.evidence_quote: Some claimed at the time that the cartel's influence was behind the M-19's raid, because of its interest in intimidating the Supreme Court. Others state that the alleged cartel-guerrilla relationship was unlikely to occur at the time because the two organizations had been having several standoffs and confrontations, like the kidnappings by M-19 of drug lord Carlos Lehder and of Marta Nieves Ochoa, the sister of Juan David Ochoa.

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel formed a vigilante group to defend against M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: the drug lords of the Medellín Cartel formed a paramilitary vigilante group known as Muerte a Secuestradores ("Death to Kidnappers") to defend the cartel against the FARC and M-19

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel retaliated against the M-19 for kidnapping Martha Nieves Ochoa, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: En represalia al secuestro de Martha Nieves Ochoa (hermana del clan Ochoa) por parte del Movimiento 19 de abril (M-19) ocurrido el 12 de noviembre de 1981; el diciembre de 1981, los hermanos Ochoa convocaron a un encuentro al que asistieron más de 200 sicarios del cartel, la mayoría de sus socios, entre ellos Pablo Escobar y Gonzalo Rodríguez Gacha, además de Carlos Lehder quien logró escapar de un intento de secuestro perpetrado también por el M-19.

descriptions: Escobar financed the 1985 Palace of Justice siege carried out by M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: She stated that Escobar had financed the operation, which was committed by M-19

descriptions: The Medellín Cartel later financed the M-19 after initial conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: se afirma que el cartel financió al M-19 debido a que después de la guerra que hubo entre estas dos organizaciones vinieron acercamientos y reuniones entre el jefe del M-19, Iván Marino Ospina y Pablo Escobar.

descriptions: MAS waged counterinsurgency warfare against M-19.
edges.evidence_quote: Muerte a Secuestradores assassinated political opponents and community organizers, and waged counterinsurgency warfare against guerrilla movements such as the FARC-EP and the M-19 .

descriptions: EPL and M-19 were allies until 1990.
edges.evidence_quote: Aliados: M-19 ( Hasta 1990 )

descriptions: Outlaws patched over the Iron Cross club in 1967, absorbing it.
edges.evidence_quote: The Outlaws further expanded into Florida in July 1967 by "patching over" the Iron Cross club in West Palm Beach.

descriptions: The Warlocks kidnapped a Breed chapter president in retaliation for assaults by Breed members.
edges.evidence_quote: In December 1988, members of the Warlocks kidnapped the Breed's Bucks County chapter president Craig "Coyote" Gudkneckt in retaliation for several Warlocks being assaulted by Breed members

descriptions: Warlocks is a smaller motorcycle gang allied with Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: The Hells Angels are allied with numerous smaller motorcycle gangs – such as the ... Warlocks

descriptions: Infobox lists Warlocks MC as rival.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Devils Diciples MC, Diablos MC, El Forastero MC, Galloping Goose MC, Hells Angels MC, Highwaymen MC, Iron Horsemen MC, Loners MC, Mongols MC, Pagans MC, Red Devils MC, Sons of Silence MC, Warlocks MC

descriptions: The Warlocks are a rival biker gang of the Outlaws.
edges.evidence_quote: Rival biker gangs of the Outlaws include the Devils Diciples, the Diablos, El Forastero, the Galloping Goose, the Hells Angels, the Highwaymen, the Iron Horsemen, the Mongols, the Pagans, the Sons of Silence, and the Warlocks

descriptions: Listed as an ally in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Warlocks MC

descriptions: Cártel del Golfo created Los Ceros as a criminal group.
edges.evidence_quote: A lo largo de los años, el CDG creó grupos criminales como Los Ciclones , Los Metros , Los Escorpiones , Los Rojos , Los Sierra o División Sierra , Los Ceros, Fuerzas Especiales Grupo Sombra (F.E.G.S.), Los Zetas , entre otros.
